scope_note: One prototype review covers all 3 sprints because they execute as a single milestone batch (autopilot one-milestone override).
design_decisions: Folded clone into the existing Open Repository modal — single drawer footer button, smart URL detection pins a 'Clone <url>' affordance at the top of the result list (user override of the original separate '+ Clone' design).
Repo deletion uses GitHub-style two-tier confirm: simple 1-step when clean, type-the-name confirm when any worktree has unpushed work (ahead commits / dirty / untracked / no-upstream).
Per-worktree breakdown in delete warning: ahead-commits / dirty / untracked categories shown separately so the user sees exactly what would be lost.
Removed slash mode from ⌘K palette entirely — the Claude tab composer already has its own slash UI; '/slash mode in palette was send-to-focused-terminal which broke when Claude was focused.

`>command` mode now resolves a Bash target instead of focused terminal: mru cached in localStorage, auto-create when none exists, Cmd+Enter opens an explicit Bash picker (palette stays in tact, just shifts result set).
Empty palette query shows recents + builtin tab actions (new bash / close current / rename / toggle theme / open on GitHub) — replaces the prior 'Start typing…' dead screen.
Added `?` prefix for content grep — distinct from `:` (path search) so the prefix legend reads cleanly.
Claude tab toolbar gets a persistent ▶ Run button — discoverable on mobile and for users who don't know ⌘K.

S032 user-commands editor uses the existing Modal popup pattern (matching Claude `.claude/settings.json` editor) instead of building a global settings page from scratch — entry point is ⌘K → `> manage user commands` builtin. Full settings page deferred to a future sprint when other settings need a UI.
All scrollbars are themed (thin, --color-fg-ghost thumb, transparent track, padding-box clip) so the prototype matches the Fog v2.1 dark surface aesthetic on both Firefox and WebKit.
